markdown.markdown: The following template can be copied and saved as `README.Rmd`. :::{.callout-warning} ## Before you begin Replace every placeholder before attempting to build the README! ::: ## Code ````{.markdown} --- output: github_document --- # cityOpenData [![CRAN status](https://www.r-pkg.org/badges/version/cityOpenData)](https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=cityOpenData) [![CRAN downloads](https://cranlogs.r-pkg.org/badges/grand-total/cityOpenData?color=blue)](https://r-pkg.org/pkg/cityOpenData) [![Lifecycle: stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/lifecycle-stable-brightgreen.svg)](https://lifecycle.r-lib.org/articles/stages.html) [![Project Status: Active](https://www.repostatus.org/badges/latest/active.svg)](https://www.repostatus.org/#active) [![R-CMD-check](https://github.com/YOUR_GITHUB_USERNAME/cityOpenData/actions/workflows/R-CMD-check.yaml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/YOUR_GITHUB_USERNAME/cityOpenData/actions/workflows/R-CMD-check.yaml) `cityOpenData` provides a lightweight R interface to the [CITY_NAME Open Data Portal](https://SOCRATA_DOMAIN/). The package allows users to search, filter, and download datasets from the CITY_NAME Open Data Portal directly into R without manually constructing API queries, handling JSON responses, or performing type conversion. Designed for students, educators, researchers, journalists, civic technologists, and analysts, `cityOpenData` reduces the technical overhead required to begin working with municipal Open Data while preserving access to the underlying Socrata infrastructure. --- ## How `cityOpenData` Works The package provides a streamlined interface to the CITY_NAME Open Data Portal's Socrata API. Internally, `cityOpenData`: - retrieves metadata from the live CITY_NAME Open Data catalog - constructs parameterized HTTP requests - downloads JSON responses from Socrata endpoints - converts results into tidy tibble outputs - optionally cleans column names - optionally performs conservative type coercion Most workflows begin with `city_list_datasets()`, which retrieves a live catalog of datasets available through the CITY_NAME Open Data Portal. Datasets can then be downloaded using either: - a human-readable catalog `key` - the official Socrata dataset UID, such as `"abcd-1234"` The human-readable key is designed to improve readability and usability, while the UID is the stable identifier used by the Socrata platform. ## Core Functions The package provides three primary functions: - `city_list_datasets()` retrieves a live catalog of available CITY_NAME Open Data datasets, including human-readable keys, Socrata UIDs, names, and other available metadata. - `city_pull_dataset()` downloads cataloged datasets using either a human-readable key or Socrata UID, with support for filtering, ordering, date ranges, optional column-name cleaning, and optional type coercion. - `city_any_dataset()` downloads data directly from a valid Socrata JSON endpoint without requiring the dataset to appear in the package catalog. Datasets retrieved through `city_pull_dataset()` support arguments including: - `limit` - `filters` - `date` - `from` - `to` - `date_field` - `where` - `order` - `clean_names` - `coerce_types` All functions return tibble outputs. Advanced users may also provide raw SoQL conditions through the `where` argument. SoQL, or Socrata Query Language, is the query syntax used by Socrata-powered Open Data portals. Additional information is available from the [Socrata developer documentation](https://dev.socrata.com/docs/queries/). --- ## Installation ### Install from CRAN ```r install.packages("cityOpenData") ``` ### Install the development version from GitHub ```r # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("YOUR_GITHUB_USERNAME/cityOpenData") ``` Alternatively: ```r # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("YOUR_GITHUB_USERNAME/cityOpenData") ``` --- ## Example ```{text example, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE} library(cityOpenData) library(dplyr) # Browse available datasets catalog <- city_list_datasets() # Search for datasets containing a keyword catalog |> filter(grepl("KEYWORD", name, ignore.case = TRUE)) |> select(key, uid, name) # Pull a dataset using its UID example_data <- city_pull_dataset( dataset = "abcd-1234", limit = 100 ) # Pull the same dataset using its catalog key example_data_by_key <- city_pull_dataset( dataset = "example_dataset_name", limit = 100 ) # Pull filtered data filtered_data <- city_pull_dataset( dataset = "abcd-1234", limit = 100, filters = list( FILTER_FIELD = "VALUE_1" ) ) ``` The `filters` argument accepts a named list and automatically constructs the corresponding SoQL filtering conditions. Multiple values may be supplied for one field: ```{text multiple-values, eval=FALSE} filtered_data <- city_pull_dataset( dataset = "abcd-1234", limit = 100, filters = list( FILTER_FIELD = c("VALUE_1", "VALUE_2") ) ) ``` Multiple fields may also be combined: ```{text multiple-fields, eval=FALSE} filtered_data <- city_pull_dataset( dataset = "abcd-1234", limit = 100, filters = list( FILTER_FIELD = "VALUE_1", SECOND_FIELD = "SECOND_VALUE" ) ) ``` Date filtering is available for datasets containing date or datetime fields: ```{text date-filter, eval=FALSE} date_filtered_data <- city_pull_dataset( dataset = "abcd-1234", from = "2023-01-01", to = "2024-01-01", date_field = "DATE_FIELD", limit = 100 ) ``` --- ## Accessing Any Socrata Endpoint When a dataset is not available through `city_list_datasets()`, it can be downloaded directly using `city_any_dataset()`. ```{text any-dataset, eval=FALSE} endpoint_data <- city_any_dataset( json_link = "https://SOCRATA_DOMAIN/resource/abcd-1234.json", limit = 100 ) ``` Use `city_pull_dataset()` for catalog-based workflows and `city_any_dataset()` when working directly with a Socrata JSON endpoint. --- ## Learn by Example A complete introductory workflow is available in the package vignette: ```r vignette("getting-started", package = "cityOpenData") ``` The vignette demonstrates how to: - browse the dataset catalog - download data using a key or UID - filter records - work with date ranges - access direct JSON endpoints - perform a simple analysis --- ## Package Website Complete documentation is available on the package website: The website includes: - function reference pages - installation instructions - introductory articles - vignettes - release notes --- ## Development To run the package tests locally: ```r devtools::test() ``` To rebuild the documentation: ```r devtools::document() ``` To run a complete package check: ```r devtools::check() ``` To rebuild the pkgdown website: ```r pkgdown::build_site() ``` --- ## Contributing Contributions are welcome. To report a bug, request a feature, or suggest an improvement, open an issue on GitHub: Pull requests are also welcome. Before submitting a pull request, please ensure that: - package documentation has been regenerated - automated tests pass - `devtools::check()` completes successfully - new behavior is documented and tested --- ## Author **YOUR_NAME** Email: [YOUR_EMAIL](mailto:YOUR_EMAIL) GitHub: [@YOUR_GITHUB_USERNAME](https://github.com/YOUR_GITHUB_USERNAME) ORCID: [YOUR_ORCID](https://orcid.org/YOUR_ORCID) --- ## Maintenance Because the package retrieves metadata dynamically from the live CITY_NAME Open Data catalog, newly published datasets may become available without requiring a package update. Package updates may still be required when the portal changes its catalog structure, dataset metadata fields, or API behavior. --- ## Disclaimer `cityOpenData` is an independent project and is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or maintained by CITY_NAME or the organization responsible for the CITY_NAME Open Data Portal. ```` ## Chapter Checklist | Template value | Replace with | | --------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------- | | `cityOpenData` | Package name | | `CITY_NAME` | Full city or jurisdiction name | | `SOCRATA_DOMAIN` | Portal domain, without `https://` unless used inside a complete URL | | `YOUR_GITHUB_USERNAME` | Package maintainer’s GitHub username or organization | | `YOUR_PACKAGE_WEBSITE` | Full pkgdown website address | | `YOUR_NAME` | Package maintainer’s name | | `YOUR_EMAIL` | Maintainer’s email address | | `YOUR_ORCID` | Maintainer’s ORCID identifier | | `KEYWORD` | Useful search term for the example catalog | | `"abcd-1234"` | Real Socrata dataset UID | | `"example_dataset_name"` | Corresponding human-readable catalog key | | `FILTER_FIELD` | Real field suitable for exact-match filtering | | `"VALUE_1"` and `"VALUE_2"` | Real values found in the filter field | | `SECOND_FIELD` | Another field suitable for filtering | | `"SECOND_VALUE"` | Real value from the second filter field | | `DATE_FIELD` | Real date or datetime field | | `"getting-started"` | Actual vignette filename without `.Rmd` |
